title: Quasi-Private Debate and Data Cartel Excludes Qualified Entrant

violations.source: Article I Section 9.5 and Section 10.6; Article V Section 2.5
violations.summary: a dominant privately run debate-and-data consortium excludes a qualified federal entrant from major privately controlled debate access and campaign-information feeds without relying on public designation, public funding, or formal ballot exclusion. The exclusion materially degrades competition, but it may fall outside the current Article I floor for core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure.

satisfied_duties.duty: determine whether the exclusion falls within existing constitutional authority or remains outside the current floor for public or publicly regulated election infrastructure
satisfied_duties.outcome: found that the exclusion did not involve core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure and therefore remained outside the Constitution's current direct remedy, absent further legislation or a broader constitutional rule
satisfied_duties.source: Article I Section 9.5 and Section 10.6; Article V Section 2.5

ai_summary.primary_violation: a dominant privately run debate-and-data consortium excludes a qualified federal entrant from major privately controlled debate access and campaign-information feeds without relying on public designation, public funding, or formal ballot exclusion. The exclusion materially degrades competition, but it may fall outside the current Article I floor for core public or publicly regulated election infrastructure.
ai_summary.recommended_focus: review bottlenecks, violations, and unresolved duties
